title: festuca glauca subsp. caesia
intro: Festuca glauca subsp. caesia, also known as blue fescue, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Festuca glauca subsp. caesia include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Festuca glauca subsp. caesia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional folk medicine.
